facts: A space elevator would need to be anchored to an object 60,000 miles long, or about 2.5 times the distance from New York to Los Angeles
It would have to withstand 100,000 tons of tension, equivalent to the weight of 200,000 cars
The cable would be roughly the width of 10 football fields if it were the same thickness as a pencil
idea.payoff: and we still don't know if it's feasible with current materials
idea.title: Space Elevator 🚀
idea.description: Imagine being able to lift objects into space without rockets 🚀! A space elevator could revolutionize space travel, but is it actually possible? 🤔 With a cable that would need to be thousands of miles long and withstand massive tension, the challenges are enormous. Can humanity make it a reality? 🚀

scenes.visual: Dark space background with a stylized Earth. A faint, impossibly long line extends from Earth's surface into space, hinting at the vastness of a space elevator.

A visual of Earth in orbit with a glowing, stylized cable stretching upwards, disappearing into the distance. A measuring line appears alongside, indicating '60,000 miles', with a smaller comparative visual of '2.5x NY to LA distance'.
An animated graphic of a segment of a cable under immense strain, with stress lines radiating. Below it, a stacked graphic of 200,000 cars representing the equivalent weight.

A visual comparison. On one side, a thin line representing a pencil-width cable. On the other side, a vast, stylized graphic of 10 football fields laid out side-by-side, showing the scale difference for equivalent strength.

scenes.text: Feasibility with current materials? Still unknown.
scenes.visual: A grand, sweeping view of a conceptual space elevator extending from Earth to a counterweight in space. A large, slightly transparent question mark shimmers over the entire structure, fading in and out.
